tickerText: [TENNIS] Wimbledon 2026 eve at the All England Club: defending champions Jannik Sinner and Iga Swiatek sharpen on the grass Sunday, June 28, ahead of Monday's main-draw opener — Sinner faces Miomir Kecmanovic on Centre Court while Swiatek meets Taylor Townsend on Tuesday; 44-year-old wildcard Serena Williams draws buzz at Aorangi Park before her Tuesday first-round clash with Australian Maya Joint • [SQUASH] Leandro Romiglio wins the Madrid Squash Open 2026 at Club de Squash Tres Cantos — the No. 5 seed edges Czech qualifier Marek Panacek 9-11, 11-7, 18-16, 11-9 in Saturday's final after Panacek's run through top seed Henry Leung and Will Salter • [BADMINTON] YONEX US Open 2026 championship Sunday at Titan Gym in Fullerton: Kidambi Srikanth rallies past Yudai Okimoto 22-20, 15-21, 21-19 to reach the men's singles final against Su Li Yang — his first BWF World Tour title match since 2017 — as five trophy matches begin at noon local time • [PICKLEBALL] Angie Walker and Anderson Scarpa complete Triple Crowns at the Portland PPA Challenger in Westbrook, Maine — singles Friday, gender doubles Saturday, and mixed doubles Sunday at The Picklr to close the June 26–28 New England stop before the Veolia Atlanta Pickleball Championships • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT United States Smash 2026 main draw opens Sunday, June 28, at the Ontario Convention Center — Round of 64 singles and doubles kick off an 11-day, $1.55M Grand Smash featuring Wang Chuqin, Sun Yingsha, Tomokazu Harimoto, Felix Lebrun, Kanak Jha, and Lily Zhang through July 5

newsItems.title: Sinner and Swiatek Fine-Tune Title Defenses on Wimbledon Eve
newsItems.excerpt: The final practice day before Wimbledon 2026, Sunday, June 28, brought defending champions Jannik Sinner and Iga Swiatek to the All England Club for last-minute grass-court tuning ahead of Monday's main-draw start. Sinner, who ramped up his physical workload after a second-round French Open exit, said he feels well prepared and will open Centre Court against Miomir Kecmanovic on Monday, June 29, when temperatures are forecast near 77°F. Swiatek, seeded No. 3, acknowledged she is keeping expectations low after limited grass preparation and an early Bad Homburg loss to Emma Navarro, and opens against Taylor Townsend on Tuesday. Meanwhile, wildcard Serena Williams drew cameras to Aorangi Park as anticipation builds for her Tuesday first-round meeting with 20-year-old Australian Maya Joint.

newsItems.title: Romiglio Clinches Madrid Squash Open Over Giant-Killer Panacek
newsItems.excerpt: Leandro Romiglio captured the Madrid Squash Open 2026 title Saturday night, June 27, at Club de Squash Tres Cantos, beating Czech qualifier Marek Panacek 9-11, 11-7, 18-16, 11-9 in the PSA 15K Challenger final. Panacek had stormed through the draw with a quarterfinal upset of top seed Henry Leung and a straight-games semifinal win over Will Salter, but Romiglio held firm after edging Spain's Sergio Garcia Pollan in a five-game semifinal of his own. The Italian No. 5 seed's victory closed a five-day event that drew 24 players to the Madrid suburbs and featured a live-music closing ceremony on finals night.

newsItems.title: Srikanth Books US Open Final Spot in Fullerton Thriller
newsItems.excerpt: Kidambi Srikanth reached the YONEX US Open 2026 men's singles final Sunday, June 28, at Titan Gym in Fullerton, California, rallying past Japan's Yudai Okimoto 22-20, 15-21, 21-19 in a 72-minute semifinal. The 32-year-old Indian veteran, a former world No. 1, will face eighth seed Su Li Yang of Chinese Taipei in the $250,000 BWF Super 300 championship match — his first tour final since November's Syed Modi International and a chance to end a title drought dating to the 2017 French Open. Earlier semifinal Saturday had seen teenagers Rounak Chouhan and Devika Sihag fall after headline Indian runs, while five finals across all disciplines begin at noon local time to close the six-day event.

newsItems.title: Walker and Scarpa Sweep Triple Crowns at Portland PPA Challenger
newsItems.excerpt: Angie Walker and Anderson Scarpa dominated the Portland PPA Challenger, June 26–28, at The Picklr Westbrook in Westbrook, Maine, each winning singles on Friday, gender doubles on Saturday — Walker with sister Alex and Scarpa with Greg Dow — and teaming up for mixed doubles gold on Sunday to complete rare Triple Crowns. Walker said the pressurized Challenger matches sharpened her game ahead of bigger stages, while Scarpa called the weekend a confidence boost before the Veolia Atlanta Pickleball Championships. Amateur brackets ran alongside the Humana Championship Court throughout the three-day New England stop on the 2026 PPA Challenger Series calendar.
